danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat requiring evasion or neutralization
Delta Corporation enforcement attempting to obstruct RoboCop's mission
Level-based threats from the urban environment and buildings
Major threat encounter requiring specialized combat tactics

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled cyborg police officer with distinctive armor
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and authority as law enforcement representative
Mission-critical non-combat entities indicating safe zones or protection objectives
Narrative safety valve providing world-state context and mission briefing

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: RoboCop cyborg armor and police authority
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban Detroit setting with police jurisdiction

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Corporate security forces and robot ninja
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed corporate security personnel; mechanized enforcer (ninja robot)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Delta City development sites; corporate-controlled urban sectors
Residential and relocation zones under threat

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story advancement between levels; player informed of objectives and world context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Armed corporate security blocking civilian zones; hostage scenarios
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens in danger; RoboCop tasked with rescue and obstruction of corporate plans

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multi-stage level design with driving, shooting, hand-to-hand combat, and jet pack sequences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player adapts to varied gameplay mechanics reflecting different threat types
Specialized combat challenge representing Delta Corporation's ultimate enforcement mechanism

environmental_storytelling.example: Movie Adventure mode allows non-linear mission selection; aborting missions affects enemy decisions and plot outcomes, creating emergent narrative consequences
In-game news segments communicate story details and world-state changes, grounding narrative progression in game's fictional media layer
Platformer and shooter segments set in Detroit's neighborhoods and corporate development zones; environment reflects Delta City expansion threat

environmental_storytelling.technique: Gameplay mechanic variety as narrative device
environmental_storytelling.example: Distinct level types (driving, shooting, hand-to-hand, jet pack, boss battles) visually represent different mission objectives and threat escalation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Traverses multi-stage levels; adapts to diverse combat and movement challenges
Engage player directly; patrol and block progression zones
Specialized combat pattern distinct from standard enemies; represents major threat escalation
Stationary or vulnerable; require player protection or extraction

notes: RoboCop 3 SNES version is a side-scrolling platformer/shooter based on 1993 film narrative: RoboCop opposes Delta Corporation's forced relocation campaign in Detroit. World-state is primarily communicated through level design variety, mission structure, and diegetic news broadcasts rather than persistent environmental markers. Political situation (corporate vs. police authority; citizen displacement) is implicit in mission objectives and faction opposition rather than explicit visual messaging. The game employs classical platformer visual grammar where enemy placement, level layout, and boss encounters signal danger and narrative progression.
